evidence_quotes.quote: protect video games, as well as other forms of media and art, from being rendered useless or inaccessible after sale to the consumer.
when a ‘live service’ game ends and becomes unavailable.
Or when an online game terminates its servers and does not allow players to create their own servers so that they can continue playing the game.

Or the fact that most digital games are simply sold as a license and you are not really the owner of the game you buy, and the company has the right to withdraw your access to the game at any time for any reason.

Finally, the most blatant example would be games that are singleplayer but to which the company has added some online elements to the game and then make the game require a continuous internet connection to their servers only in order to be able to perform (the game).
So, when they finally and inevitably decide to close their servers, you lose your access to a singleplayer game.

paragraphs.text: As a citizen of the European Union, I believe that consumer rights are something that needs to be very strongly protected. For this reason, I believe that a provision should be added to the Digital Fairness Act to protect video games, as well as other forms of media and art, from being rendered useless or inaccessible after sale to the consumer. For example, when a ‘live service’ game ends and becomes unavailable. Or when an online game terminates its servers and does not allow players to create their own servers so that they can continue playing the game. Or the fact that most digital games are simply sold as a license and you are not really the owner of the game you buy, and the company has the right to withdraw your access to the game at any time for any reason. Finally, the most blatant example would be games that are singleplayer but to which the company has added some online elements to the game and then make the game require a continuous internet connection to their servers only in order to be able to perform (the game). So, when they finally and inevitably decide to close their servers, you lose your access to a singleplayer game. This is a cartoon. To close my comments, I would just like to mention that so far I have only mentioned examples of video games because it is the most interesting to me, and also because this is a much bigger problem in this sector, I believe that there should be similar protections for all forms of digital media, such as music, films, etc. As these bad practices harming consumers could spread to other sectors in the future, protective measures should be put in place now before this happens.
